summary: High‑energy second set shines with Iko > Wheel and stellar Wharf Rat
blurb: The show delivers a punchy second‑set sequence highlighted by the Iko > Wheel jam, a soaring Wharf Rat and a bright Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s vocals waver on the extended Terrapin Station, but the band stays tight through the jams. Overall the performance is high‑energy with a few rough spots.
review: October 7, 1977 at the University of New Mexico finds the Dead in top form during the second set, launching from “Terrapin Station” into an electrifying “Playing In The Band” that segues into a tight drum break and bursts onto the iconic Iko > Wheel jam. Reviewers rave about the “out‑of‑this‑world” Jerry solos on Wharf Rat and the crisp, soaring vibe of Sugar Magnolia, while the pristine soundboard capture praised as “crystal‑clear” makes every nuance audible. The extended Terrapin Station, however, feels tentative—Jerry’s vocal delivery is described as uncertain and the song’s transitions awkward—yet Phil’s bass adds a rain‑like shimmer early on, keeping momentum alive. Drum work from Mickey and Billy drives the high‑energy moments, though occasional dropouts are noted in some recordings. In sum, this show offers a memorable mix of soaring jams and minor imperfections, making it a solid pick for fans craving that late‑’70s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across three recordings the sentiment is mixed with one clearly positive review. Positive remarks focus on the Iko > Wheel jam, Wharf Rat solos and pristine soundboard quality, while negatives note tentative Terrapin Station vocals and occasional performance hiccups. The balance yields a modest +0.6 boost.

best_recording.reason: Good-quality soundboard capture of the full second set with clear, balanced mix; highlights Iko > Wheel and Wharf Rat while preserving the show's high energy.

key_highlights: Iko > Wheel jam delivers a tight, high‑energy improvisation.
Wharf Rat features out‑of‑this‑world Jerry solos and an inventive riff.
Terrapin Station feels tentative with uncertain vocals.
Soundboard recordings range from good to pristine, giving excellent clarity.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers stellar, out‑of‑this‑world solos on Wharf Rat and strong lead work, but vocal delivery is uneven on Terrapin Station and some mid‑set numbers.
band_performance.Phil: Provides buoyant, rain‑like bass lines early in the set, adding depth to the jam flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Keeps a tight, driving pulse throughout especially during Iko > Wheel, despite occasional drop‑outs.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in solidly on high‑energy sections; minor inconsistencies noted on early songs.
